mcqs.question: Which asymptotic notation describes the tight bound of an algorithm's growth rate?
mcqs.explanation: Theta notation (Θ) provides both upper and lower bounds, describing the tight bound of an algorithm's growth rate.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of accessing an element in an array by index?
mcqs.explanation: Array access by index is a constant-time operation because it involves simple arithmetic to calculate the memory address.

mcqs.question: Which of the following represents the worst-case time complexity of binary search?
mcqs.explanation: Binary search has O(log n) time complexity in both worst and average cases, as it halves the search space with each comparison.

mcqs.question: If an algorithm has time complexity O(2ⁿ), what happens when the input size doubles?

options.text: Running time increases by a factor of 4ⁿ

mcqs.explanation: For O(2ⁿ), doubling the input size changes n to 2n, so the running time becomes 2²ⁿ = (2ⁿ)², meaning it squares itself.

mcqs.question: What is the space complexity of the recursive Fibonacci algorithm that calls fib(n-1) + fib(n-2)?
mcqs.explanation: The recursive Fibonacci algorithm has O(n) space complexity due to the maximum depth of the recursion stack, not the number of function calls.
